ecutable , Instantiable , ModelErrorHandler , MoMLExportable , Moveable , Nameable , java.io.Serializable, TypedActor public class RemoveNilTokens extends Transformer Read in tokens and silently discard any tokens that are marked as nil. Non-nill t

rd any tokens that are marked as nil. Non-nill tokens are transferred to the output. In the real world, data sources sometimes pass null values when data sources are sparsely populated. The null values are sometimes called missing values. The P

alues are sometimes called missing values. The Ptolemy II expression language represents the missing or null tokens with the string "nil". This actor should not be used in the SDF domain because the number of tokens sent to the output depends on

he number of tokens sent to the output depends on the input. Since: Ptolemy II 5.2 Version: $Id: RemoveNilTokens.java,v 1.9 2006/08/21 23:53:16 cxh Exp $ Author: Christopher Brooks See Also: Token.isNil() , Token.NIL , Serialized Form Accepted Rating

lActionException Construct an actor with the given container and name. Parameters: container - The container. name - The name of this actor. Throws: IllegalActionException - If the actor cannot be contained by the proposed container. NameDuplicati

tained by the proposed container. NameDuplicationException - If the container already has an actor with this name. Method Detail fire public void fire () throws IllegalActionException Read tokens from the input and output all non-nil

Read tokens from the input and output all non-nil (non-missing) tokens. If we get a nil (aka missing) token, then the token is read, but no output is sent. Thus, this actor cannot be used in the SDF domain. Specified by: fire in interface Exe

he SDF domain. Specified by: fire in interface Executable Overrides: fire in class AtomicActor Throws: IllegalActionException - If there is no director. Overview Package Class Tree Deprecated Index Help PREV CLASS NEXT CLASS FRAMES NO FRAMES All Clas
